English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Roger Morse papers
Morse, Roger A.
21-23-4122
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1910-2000 Correspondence, course material, and photographs Includes material related to beekeeping, his membership in the Empire State Honey Producers' Association, and Morse's career in the Department of Entomology. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 1++ ======= 1++ >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 8515369
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
December 2017
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, December 2017
Date modified:
Marcie Farwell, May 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Morse was a bee biologist in the Department of Entomology. He received his B.A. in 1950, his master's degree in 1953, and his PhD in 1955, all from Cornell.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes correspondence, course material and photographs related to beekeeping, his membership in the Empire State Honey Producers' Association, and Morse's career in the Department of Entomology. Also included is a bound volume titled "Burma and Nepal programs and reports," consisting of typewritten documents and correspondence concerning projects that Morse was involved with there. Its spine title is Morse--Asian journey--1982.)
SERIES LIST
Series I. Photographs
Series II. Teaching
Series III. Empire State Honey Producers Association
Cite As:
Roger Morse papers, #21-23-4122. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Bee culture Bees Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University. Dept. of Entomology Empire State Honey Producers\' Association
